# faBolus — LLM guide

> A pump-agnostic remote-bolus + status app for Tandem t:slim X2 / Mobi. The iPhone owns the pump's
> Bluetooth link (via the sibling `PumpX2Kit` package); the Apple Watch, Garmin, Mac, and a second
> iPhone are thin remotes that relay confirmed commands to the host phone. Experimental, not
> FDA-cleared. This file orients an LLM helping either a **user** set up/use the app or a **developer**
> add features / debug. Paths are repo-relative; every file has a doc-comment header — read it first.

## Safety invariants (never violate)
- Every insulin-affecting command goes: UI confirm/hold → backend clamp (`Interlocks.absoluteMaxUnits`
  = 25 U, ≥ 0.05 U) → transport `WritePolicy` (`.readOnly` default) → signed + `modifiesInsulinDelivery`
  message, byte-verified against the `PumpX2Kit` oracle. Don't add a delivery path that skips these.
- Stale glucose (> ~6 min, `GlucoseFreshness`) is shown marked, never as current, and never auto-fills
  a correction.
- Remote/parental gates are enforced in `AppModel` (the single chokepoint) — see "Permissions".

## Architecture (start here)
- [docs/architecture.md](docs/architecture.md) — how it fits together + the command contract.
- `Packages/faBolusCore/` — backend-neutral core: `PumpBackend` (pump seam), `GlucoseSource`/
  `GlucoseArbiter` (CGM failover), `RemoteCommand` (the remote wire contract), transports
  (`RemoteLink` = WatchConnectivity, `BLELink` = Mac/iPhone BLE, `SealedTransport` = AES-GCM),
  `MacPairing` (auth), `Models.swift` (`PumpSnapshot`, `PumpAlert`, …). Pure + unit-tested.
- `ios/faBolus/Data/` — app state + drivers: `AppModel` (bridges a `PumpBackend` to SwiftUI; the
  command + gating chokepoint), `TandemBackend` (real pump via PumpX2Kit), `MockBackend` (Simulator),
  `PeerRemoteHost`/`PhoneRemoteHost`/`GarminRemoteBridge` (remote hosts), `AppSettings`, `ChildMode`.
- `ios/faBolus/Views/` — SwiftUI. Display views take plain values (reused by remotes).
- `Shared/` — cross-target: `RemoteClientModel` (dumb-remote base), CGM BLE sources, widget store.
- `watch/`, `mac/`, `ios/faBolusWidgets/` — the remotes + widgets. `garmin` app is a separate repo.

## The pump protocol lives in a sibling repo
- `../PumpX2Kit` — Tandem BLE/JPAKE/messages, byte-exact against a vendored Java oracle. faBolus
  depends on it. Change pump message bytes there (with an oracle-parity test), not here.

## Add a feature / debug — where things are
- New user setting: add to `ios/faBolus/Data/AppSettings.swift` (UserDefaults + `didSet`, sanitized in
  `init`) and a `*SettingsView` in `ios/faBolus/Views/SettingsView.swift` (+ a `SettingsIndex` entry).
- New pump action: add to the `PumpBackend` protocol (`Packages/faBolusCore/.../PumpBackend.swift`,
  default-throwing extension), implement in `TandemBackend` + `MockBackend`, expose via `AppModel`.
- New remote command: extend `RemoteCommand` + `schema/command.schema.json` (run
  `scripts/check-schema-drift.sh`); handle in the `*RemoteHost` receivers + `RemoteClientModel`.
- Permissions (child mode / remote peers): `ChildFeature` / `RemotePermission`; enforced by
  `AppModel.childBlocked` / `PeerRemoteHost` — the one place to gate actions.

## Build / test / run (commands)
- Core tests: `swift test --package-path Packages/faBolusCore`.
- Simulator build: `./scripts/build-sim.sh` (iOS app + embedded watch + widgets).
- Device build: set `DEVELOPMENT_TEAM` in `LocalConfig.xcconfig`; `xcodegen generate` then
  `xcodebuild -scheme faBolus -destination 'id=<UDID>' -allowProvisioningUpdates build`; install with
  `xcrun devicectl device install app`.
- Schema drift: `scripts/check-schema-drift.sh`. Docs site: mkdocs (`mkdocs.yml`).

## Docs (for users)
- Site: https://fabolus.org/ · sources in [docs/](docs/). Start: [docs/safety.md](docs/safety.md),
  [docs/build/index.md](docs/build/index.md), [docs/operate/status.md](docs/operate/status.md).
- Remotes: [Apple Watch](docs/remotes/apple-watch.md), [Garmin](docs/remotes/garmin.md),
  [Mac](docs/remotes/mac.md), [iPhone remote](docs/remotes/phone-remote.md).
- Roadmap / deferred + known caveats: [ROADMAP.md](ROADMAP.md),
  [docs/UNVERIFIED-GUESSES.md](docs/UNVERIFIED-GUESSES.md).

## Conventions
- Match surrounding style; every file starts with a doc-comment explaining its role — keep that up.
- `[[double-bracket]]` links in doc-comments point to sibling types/files.
- Don't invent pump behavior; if unverified, note it in `docs/UNVERIFIED-GUESSES.md`.
